Description: AFI is a clinical syndrome characterized by a sudden onset of fever due to underlying infectious or inflammatory causes of viral, bacterial, or parasitic origin.
AGE is an inflammatory condition of the gastrointestinal tract involving the stomach and intestines, primarily caused by infectious agents such as viruses, bacteria, or protozoa.
I/P is a disease condition caused by helminths or protozoan organisms that inhabit the human gastrointestinal tract and disrupt normal physiological processes.
URTI is an infectious disease affecting the upper respiratory system including the nasal cavity, pharynx, and larynx, usually caused by viral or bacterial pathogens.
